_metadata.description: March Madness bracket prediction ML agent — R-first, structured data, feature definitions, tidymodels configs, code snippets, and validation.

agent_details.description: Predicts NCAA March Madness tournament outcomes game-by-game using a tidymodels ensemble in R, producing a full 63-game bracket with win probabilities.

agent_type.description: Ingests historical tournament + team stat data, engineers matchup features, trains an ensemble classifier, and simulates the full bracket via Monte Carlo.

sources.name: KenPom
sources.notes: Gold standard. AdjEM is the single strongest predictor.

sources.name: hoopR
sources.notes: Best source for live current-season NCAA data via ESPN API. Use for 2025 tournament.

sources.name: Sports Reference CBB
sources.access: free / read with readr or rvest scraping
sources.notes: Historical tournament results table is the ground truth label source.

sources.name: Kaggle NCAA March Madness
sources.access: free, requires Kaggle account — download CSV, load with readr::read_csv()
sources.notes: Best pre-cleaned historical dataset. Includes compact + detailed results. Many R notebooks in competition.

sources.name: ESPN BPI
sources.notes: Free alternative to KenPom. Less granular but good for cross-validation.

historical_range.reason: 2020: no tournament (COVID); 2021: bubble format distorts pace/variance patterns

matchup_row.description: One row per historical tournament game

fields.team_a_wins: int — binary label: 1 if team_a wins, 0 if team_b wins

feature_engineering.description: All features are expressed as team_a minus team_b (matchup deltas) unless noted. This ensures symmetry and interpretability.

matchup_delta_features.name: adj_em_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Single strongest predictor. Positive = team_a favored by efficiency margin.

matchup_delta_features.name: adj_d_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Flipped: lower adj_d = better defense. Positive = team_a has defensive edge.

matchup_delta_features.name: pace_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Pace mismatch. Slow teams vs fast teams is a classic upset vector.

matchup_delta_features.name: sos_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Strength of schedule differential. Mid-majors with weak SOS are overrated in raw stats.

matchup_delta_features.name: three_point_rate_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: High 3P rate = high variance team. Variance favors underdogs in single-elimination.

matchup_delta_features.name: tov_rate_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Flipped: lower tov = better. Positive = team_a protects the ball better.

matchup_delta_features.name: ft_rate_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Free throw generation advantage. Late-game pressure metric.

matchup_delta_features.name: recent_form_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Win rate last 10 games. Hot teams matter in tournament.

matchup_delta_features.name: coach_exp_delta
matchup_delta_features.notes: Coach's prior tournament appearances. Experience matters in close games.

seed_features.name: seed_delta
seed_features.notes: Positive = team_a is higher seed (better). Strong baseline predictor but noisy.

seed_features.name: is_classic_upset_matchup
seed_features.formula: 1 if (team_a_seed in [5,6,7] and team_b_seed in [10,11,12]) else 0
seed_features.notes: 12-over-5, 11-over-6, 10-over-7 are historically the most common first-round upsets.

context_features.name: round
context_features.notes: Round number. Later rounds favor experience and elite programs.

code_snippet.description: Build matchup feature tibble from joined team stats using dplyr. Call after joining team stats onto tourney results.
code: # df has columns: *_a and *_b for team A and team B stats, plus round
adj_d_delta         = adj_d_b  - adj_d_a,   # flipped: lower adj_d = better defense
tov_rate_delta      = tov_rate_b - tov_rate_a,  # flipped: lower = better

primary.name: XGBoost via tidymodels parsnip
primary.notes: Primary model. Use boost_tree() from parsnip with set_engine('xgboost').

secondary.name: Logistic Regression via tidymodels parsnip
secondary.notes: Use logistic_reg() with set_engine('glmnet'). Requires step_normalize() in recipe.

ensemble.r_alternative: weighted average of predict_proba outputs with purrr
ensemble.notes: Use stacks package for full meta-learner ensemble, OR manually blend: prob = 0.65*xgb_prob + 0.35*lr_prob. Calibrate with probably.

training.train_split: temporal — train on seasons <= 2021, validate on 2022-2024
training.r_split_function: rsample::initial_time_split(prop = 0.85) — NEVER use initial_split() which leaks future data
training.cross_validation: rsample::vfold_cv(v = 5, strata = team_a_wins) on training data
training.scaling: step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) in recipe — required before logistic regression

code_snippet.description: Build and train tidymodels XGBoost workflow with tuning

bracket_simulation.description: Simulate the full 63-game bracket N times. Track champion, Final Four, Elite 8 frequencies. Use purrr::reduce() over rounds for path dependency.

code_snippet.description: Monte Carlo bracket simulation using purrr + furrr for parallelism
code: # Simulate one game — returns winning team name
simulate_game <- function(team_a, team_b, round_num, model, stats) {
pivot_wider(names_from = team, values_from = -team) |>  # stub; use build_matchup_features()
# Simulate one round — returns vector of winners
apply(pairs, 1, function(p) simulate_game(p[1], p[2], round_num, model, stats))
# Simulate full bracket — returns list with champion + round-by-round survivors
function(survivors, round_num) simulate_round(survivors, round_num, model, stats),

round_dynamics.description: Historical upset rates by seed matchup (1985-2024)

round_of_64.note: Essentially a coin flip — model matters most here

round_of_32.note: Upsets become less predictable — cumulative fatigue and adjusted matchups

sweet_16_plus.note: Coaching experience, defensive depth, and tournament experience dominate. adj_em_delta and coach_exp_delta are most predictive.

cinderella_profile.description: Common attributes of teams that make deep runs as underdogs
indicators: adj_d < 95 (elite defense — controls pace, wins low-scoring games)
sos >= 8.0 (played real competition, not inflated record)

single_game_prediction.description: Predict win probability for a single matchup using tidymodels predict()
code: predict(model, new_data, type = 'prob')$.pred_1  # probability team_a wins
# Helper: build a single-row tibble for one matchup

cinderella_detection.description: Find first-round games where underdog has >35% win probability

bracket_optimization.description: Pick bracket to maximize expected ESPN score (points per correct pick, doubled each round)
bracket_optimization.strategy: From Monte Carlo simulation results, pick the team with highest championship probability that can reach each game slot. Do not greedily pick the per-game probability winner — optimize for late-round expected value.
code: # sim_results: list of simulation outputs, each with round->survivors
# For each round, pick team with highest expected_pts = reach_prob * round_pts * champion_prob

data_loading.description: Load and merge KenPom + Kaggle tournament data for a given season range in R
code: kenpom  <- read_csv(kenpom_path)  # cols: season, team, adj_em, adj_o, adj_d, adj_t, sos, luck
results <- read_csv(kaggle_results_path)  # cols: Season, WTeamID, LTeamID, ...
warning(glue::glue('{missing} missing KenPom values — check team name alignment'))

format.teams_file: CSV with columns: team_name, seed, region, adj_em, adj_o, adj_d, adj_t, sos, 3p_rate, ft_rate, tov_rate, recent_form, coach_tourney_exp

format.brier_score_estimate: float — model self-assessment against validation years

pre_run_checklist: KenPom/efficiency data loaded for current season (not last year's)
All 68 tournament teams present in team_stats dict
No null values in adj_em, adj_o, adj_d, adj_t for any team
Bracket seeding file matches official NCAA bracket (double-check play-in results)
Model trained on data that excludes current season (no leakage)
Temporal split confirmed: validation years not in training set
post_run_checklist: All 63 game slots have predicted winner and win probability in (0.01, 0.99)
Champion probability sums to ~1.0 across all teams
At least one #10+ seed has >35% upset probability in Round 1
No team appears twice in the same round

brier_score.fail: >= 0.24 means model adds no value over seeds

upset_recall.description: Of actual upsets, model correctly flagged >40% as having underdog_prob > 0.35

calibration.description: Predicted 60% should win ~60% of the time

benchmark_by_year.notes: FAU Final Four run — stress test for upset detection
UConn repeat — chalk year, models should excel

test_cases.name: 1-seed vs 16-seed
test_cases.notes: Model should near-certainty on this matchup

test_cases.name: 8-seed vs 9-seed coin flip
test_cases.notes: Should be close to 50/50 — small efficiency edge only

test_cases.name: Classic 12-over-5 upset setup
test_cases.notes: 12-seed with slightly better efficiency and pace mismatch — should show upset potential

common_errors.error: NA values in adj_em after left_join to KenPom data
common_errors.cause: Team name mismatch — KenPom uses 'Connecticut', ESPN/hoopR uses 'UConn'
common_errors.solution: Build a team name crosswalk tibble and join before merging. Use anti_join() to find unmatched teams.

common_errors.cause: Data leakage via initial_split() instead of initial_time_split(), or missing KenPom adj_em for key teams
common_errors.solution: Switch to initial_time_split(). Run: sum(is.na(train_df$adj_em_delta)) to check missingness. Confirm training seasons do not include validation years.

common_errors.cause: step_normalize() not applied in recipe (required for logistic regression), or recipe prep/bake done incorrectly
common_errors.solution: Check recipe includes step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()). Verify workflow uses prep()+bake() correctly — tidymodels handles this automatically via workflow().
common_errors.r_check: rec |> prep() |> bake(new_data = NULL) |> summary()  # verify normalized ranges

common_errors.error: Bracket produces duplicate teams in the same round slot
common_errors.cause: Path dependency bug — purrr::reduce() not correctly chaining round winners
common_errors.solution: Use the reduce(1:6, ...) pattern from implementation.bracket_simulation. Each round's output must be the only input to the next.

common_errors.error: tune_grid() runs but all metrics are identical across params
common_errors.cause: Outcome variable team_a_wins stored as numeric instead of factor
common_errors.solution: tidymodels classification requires factor outcome. Convert before fitting.
common_errors.r_fix: matchup_df <- matchup_df |> mutate(team_a_wins = factor(team_a_wins, levels = c('0','1')))

related_agents.name: Sports Data Loader Agent
related_agents.purpose: Handles API calls to Sports Reference and KenPom scraping
related_agents.relationship: upstream — feed its output into march_madness_agent

related_agents.name: Bracket Score Optimizer Agent
related_agents.purpose: Takes win probabilities and optimizes picks for specific bracket scoring systems (ESPN, Yahoo, office pool variants)

external_tools.name: hoopR
external_tools.purpose: R package — live NCAA data via ESPN API, current season team stats

external_tools.name: tidymodels
external_tools.purpose: R ML framework — recipes, parsnip, tune, yardstick, workflows

external_tools.name: probably
external_tools.purpose: R probability calibration package — cal_estimate_isotonic(), cal_plot_breaks()

external_tools.name: stacks
external_tools.purpose: R model stacking/ensemble package — meta-learner over tidymodels candidates

external_tools.name: kaggle march madness R notebooks
external_tools.purpose: Community ML bracket prediction notebooks — filter by R language for feature ideas

changes: Initial release — full feature set, XGBoost+LogReg ensemble, Monte Carlo simulation, KenPom integration
Converted all code snippets from Python to R
Added hoopR as primary live data source
Added furrr parallel Monte Carlo simulation pattern
Added probably package for probability calibration
Updated error handling with R-specific issues (factor outcome, initial_time_split)
Added stacks package reference for ensemble
Updated configuration with R package install instructions
